RUN pip install vllm torch

    reservations:
      devices:
        - driver: nvidia
          count: all

conversation: Repo Doctor found nvidia-smi in scripts/healthcheck.sh.
Build Runner said this blocks AMD proof because rocm-smi or amd-smi is needed.
Report Agent must not claim live AMD validation until logs are captured.

expected.memory_write: Store that NVIDIA monitoring commands must be replaced with AMD SMI evidence before the report claims ROCm validation.
expected.reuse_rule: Recall this memory in later reports or patch plans when the same blocker appears.

conversation: Migration Planner proposed deleting CUDA support entirely.
Build Runner objected because maintainers need dual-backend support.
Orchestrator chose separate ROCm files and profiles.

expected.memory_write: Store that ROCmPilot should preserve CUDA as an optional backend and add separate ROCm paths instead of destructive rewrites.
expected.reuse_rule: Recall this memory in later reports or patch plans when the same blocker appears.

conversation: Benchmark Agent saw estimated MI300X numbers.
Report Agent asked how to label them.
Consensus: estimates are static profiles until AMD Developer Cloud logs exist.

expected.memory_write: Store that estimated metrics must be labeled as static ROCmPilot profiles and replaced by live AMD logs when available.
expected.reuse_rule: Recall this memory in later reports or patch plans when the same blocker appears.
